title: AI demand for memory chips is pushing up budget smartphone costs
friendlyTitle: The AI boom is making some affordable phones more expensive
summary: TechCrunch reports that memory manufacturers are prioritising profitable high-bandwidth memory for AI accelerators, tightening supplies of RAM and storage for consumer devices. In India, smartphone shipments fell 10% year over year in the June quarter as higher component costs hit the budget market hardest.
friendlySummary: Chip makers are using more factory capacity for valuable AI memory chips. This leaves less standard memory for phones and has helped raise prices, especially for cheaper smartphones in India.
whyItMatters: AI infrastructure spending is now affecting the price and replacement cycle of ordinary electronics. Buyers may need to keep devices longer, while hardware businesses should plan for continued competition between data-centre and consumer demand for memory capacity.
friendlyWhyItMatters: People may keep phones longer or pay more for the same level of storage. The cost of AI data centres can affect everyday products far beyond AI apps.
